channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: Prestigious Grants 2026: Sir Halley Stewart Trust Funding (Up to £60,000)
item.dc:creator: Michelle Zyongwe
item.description:
Grants are now open through the Sir Halley Stewart Trust for UK-based charities and educational institutions seeking funding between £5,000 and £60,000 for innovative religious, social, and medical projects. Applications for the next main funding round must be submitted by 29 June 2026. The Sir Halley Stewart Trust is a Christian-based foundation committed to supporting […]

The post Prestigious Grants 2026: Sir Halley Stewart Trust Funding (Up to £60,000) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Grants are now open through the Sir Halley Stewart Trust for UK-based charities and educational institutions seeking funding between £5,000 and £60,000 for innovative religious, social, and medical projects. Applications for the next main funding round must be submitted by 29 June 2026.

The Sir Halley Stewart Trust is a Christian-based foundation committed to supporting projects that promote the development of body, mind, and spirit, encourage justice and fairness, and foster international goodwill. Through its 2026 Grants programme, the Trust continues its long-standing mission of investing in initiatives that focus on prevention rather than simply alleviating human suffering.

The Trust particularly prioritizes pioneering, ground-breaking, and innovative projects that can deliver sustainable impact within communities. Whether addressing social injustice, advancing medical research, or strengthening religious dialogue, applicants must demonstrate originality and transformative potential.

About the Grants

The Sir Halley Stewart Trust supports projects across three primary funding streams: Religious, Social, and Medical.

All Grants awarded must demonstrate innovation and meet at least one of the following criteria:

  • Explore and test new ideas, methods, interventions, or devices.
  • Represent pioneering developments that lay foundations for future work.
  • Offer preventive solutions rather than reactive support.
  • Deliver impact beyond immediate beneficiaries.

The Trust strongly favors imaginative proposals and often supports early-career researchers or leaders with whom it can build a long-term relationship.

Projects must be UK-based and administered through UK charitable organizations or educational institutions.

Funding Levels

There are two main categories of Grants available:

Main Grants

  • Between £5,000 and £60,000
  • Normally awarded for salary costs
  • Can be spread across 1 to 3 years
  • Maximum annual allowance: £30,000
  • In exceptional cases, grants up to £80,000 may be awarded

Small Grants

  • Up to £5,000
  • Designed for one-off projects
  • Accepted on a rolling basis

The Trust typically prefers to fund at least 50% of total project costs, though match funding is generally not required.

Focus Areas

Religious Grants

Religious Grants prioritize innovative Christian and multi-faith projects that:

  • Break down religious and secular divides
  • Encourage dialogue between faith and contemporary issues
  • Improve inter-faith relationships
  • Promote Christian engagement in public life
  • Develop spiritual exploration and vocational leadership

Special interest is given to improving dialogue between Christian and Muslim communities and encouraging ecumenical collaboration within the UK and internationally.

Social Grants

Social Grants aim to support under-funded causes and organizations serving disadvantaged communities.

Priority areas include:

  • Preventing and resolving conflict
  • Promoting reconciliation within families and communities
  • Addressing unemployment, crime, imprisonment, homelessness, migration, and mental health challenges
  • Supporting vulnerable populations, especially the elderly

Projects must:

  • Involve beneficiaries in project design and governance
  • Demonstrate sustainability beyond the grant period
  • Show measurable impact beyond immediate beneficiaries
  • Be service-delivery charities (annual income up to £3 million)

The Trust also funds select projects in politically stable African countries through UK-administered charities.

Medical Grants

Medical Grants are awarded to practical research projects capable of clinical application within 5–10 years.

Priority areas include:

  • Improving quality of life for the elderly
  • Preventing disease and disability in children
  • Addressing tropical infectious diseases
  • Advancing healthcare innovations
  • Supporting disadvantaged populations through medical innovation

Medical projects must:

  • Be written in accessible language for non-medical trustees
  • Have Ethics Committee approval (where required)
  • Be administered through UK-based charitable institutions

The Trust particularly welcomes projects that may struggle to secure funding from major research councils.

Eligibility

Applicants must:

  • Be a registered UK charity or educational establishment
  • Submit proposals directly from individuals involved in the project
  • Demonstrate innovative and preventive impact
  • Ensure the Trust is a significant funder of the project

The Trust does not fund:

  • Running costs of established organizations
  • Large well-funded charities
  • Building construction or capital projects
  • University overhead charges
  • Conference attendance
  • Direct funding to individuals

Duration & Location

  • Funding duration: 1–3 years
  • Location: United Kingdom (some projects may operate internationally if administered by UK charities)
  • Maximum grant: £60,000 (exceptionally £80,000)

How to Apply

There are three stages to the application process:

  1. Complete the Eligibility Test.
  2. Submit an Expression of Interest (EOI) form if eligible.
  3. If invited, submit a Full Application through the online portal.

Applicants are strongly encouraged to contact the Grants Manager using the online expression of interest form before beginning the full application process.

Main Grant applications are currently accepted until 29 June 2026. Small Grant applications are accepted on a rolling basis.

APPLY HERE

 

Deadline

29 June 2026

Final Note

The Sir Halley Stewart Trust Grants provide a unique opportunity for UK-based charities and institutions to secure funding for bold, innovative, and preventive projects in religious, social, and medical fields.

Organizations committed to addressing root causes of inequality, improving healthcare, fostering interfaith dialogue, and supporting vulnerable communities are strongly encouraged to explore this funding opportunity.

For more opportunities click here

 

The post Prestigious Grants 2026: Sir Halley Stewart Trust Funding (Up to £60,000) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Apply Now: UN Human Rights Council Special Rapporteur Vacancies (HRC62, June–July 2026)
item.dc:creator: Anna Gurung
item.description:
The United Nations Human Rights Council (HRC) has officially opened applications for several prestigious Special Rapporteur mandates to be appointed during its 62nd session (HRC62), taking place 15 June – 10 July 2026. This is a rare and highly respected opportunity for internationally recognized experts in human rights to serve as independent mandate holders under […]

The post Apply Now: UN Human Rights Council Special Rapporteur Vacancies (HRC62, June–July 2026) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The United Nations Human Rights Council (HRC) has officially opened applications for several prestigious Special Rapporteur mandates to be appointed during its 62nd session (HRC62), taking place 15 June – 10 July 2026.

This is a rare and highly respected opportunity for internationally recognized experts in human rights to serve as independent mandate holders under the UN Special Procedures system.

Open Mandates – HRC62 (Apply Now)

Applications are currently being accepted for the following mandates:

  • Special Rapporteur on Toxics and Human Rights (SR Toxics)

  • Special Rapporteur on Freedom of Expression (SR FreeDex)

  • Special Rapporteur on the Right to Health (SR Health)

  • Special Rapporteur on Torture (SR Torture)

These independent experts are appointed by the Human Rights Council to examine, monitor, advise, and publicly report on human rights situations worldwide.

About the Special Procedures System

The Special Procedures are the largest body of independent experts in the UN human rights system. Mandate holders:

  • Conduct country visits

  • Respond to allegations of violations

  • Issue communications to governments

  • Present thematic and country reports to the HRC and the UN General Assembly

Mandate holders serve in their personal capacity and are not UN staff. They are expected to uphold independence, impartiality, integrity, and expertise.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Serving as a Special Rapporteur:

  • Positions you among the most respected global human rights experts

  • Allows you to influence international human rights standards

  • Enables direct engagement with governments, civil society, and victims

  • Provides a global platform to advocate for systemic change

This is one of the highest-impact voluntary roles in international human rights governance.

Selection Process

Appointments are made by the Human Rights Council following:

  1. Public call for applications

  2. Review by the Consultative Group

  3. Shortlisting and interviews

  4. Final appointment by the Council

The process emphasizes expertise, experience, independence, gender balance, and equitable geographic representation.

Application Deadline

Applications for appointments to be made at HRC62 (15 June – 10 July 2026) are currently open. Applicants should apply as soon as possible via the official OHCHR nomination and appointment portal before the closing date indicated on the application page.

Interested candidates must follow the official application guidelines and submit all required documentation through the UN system.

For detailed guidelines and application procedures, visit the official page of the United Nations Human Rights Council.

Full details on eligibility, selection criteria, and the application process (available in all six UN official languages): Click Here

Find more job opportunities here

The post Apply Now: UN Human Rights Council Special Rapporteur Vacancies (HRC62, June–July 2026) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: IBRO Collaborative Research Grants 2026: Up to $6,000 to Fund International Neuroscience Collaborations
item.dc:creator: Anna Gurung
item.description:
The International Brain Research Organization (IBRO) has officially opened applications for its Collaborative Research Grants 2026, designed to foster international partnerships between emerging and established neuroscience research groups. This competitive grant supports early-career principal investigators (PIs) seeking to build impactful scientific collaborations across regions. If you are leading an emerging laboratory and looking to expand […]

The post IBRO Collaborative Research Grants 2026: Up to $6,000 to Fund International Neuroscience Collaborations appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The International Brain Research Organization (IBRO) has officially opened applications for its Collaborative Research Grants 2026, designed to foster international partnerships between emerging and established neuroscience research groups.

This competitive grant supports early-career principal investigators (PIs) seeking to build impactful scientific collaborations across regions. If you are leading an emerging laboratory and looking to expand your research network globally, this funding opportunity can help accelerate your scientific career.

Application Deadline

Applications are open from 15 February 2026 to 15 March 2026.
All applications must be submitted through the official IBRO Grants Portal during this period.

About the IBRO Collaborative Research Grants

The IBRO Collaborative Research Grants aim to strengthen global neuroscience research by encouraging partnerships between:

  • Two emerging research groups, or

  • One emerging and one established research group

The funding supports direct collaboration expenses only, including:

  • International travel

  • Accommodation

  • Subsistence

  • Research-related expenses

Indirect costs and institutional overheads are not covered.

Collaborations must begin in 2026 and be completed within two years of the start date.

Maximum Grant Amount by Region

  • Asia-Pacific: USD 6,000

  • Africa: USD 6,000

  • Latin America: USD 6,000

  • Pan-Europe: USD 5,000

  • US/Canada: USD 6,000

Funds are disbursed using an 80:20 format:

  • 80% awarded two months before the collaboration start date

  • 20% released upon submission of the final funding report

Who Can Apply?

Project Leader Requirements

  • Must be a Principal Investigator (PI) for a maximum of 7 years

  • Must lead an emerging laboratory (established within the last 7 years)

  • Career breaks (e.g., maternity, paternity, adoption leave) are accepted if justified

Collaborator Requirements

  • Must be a Principal Investigator

  • Can be from an emerging or established laboratory

Collaboration Conditions

  • Must include bi-directional travel between groups

  • May involve PIs, postdoctoral researchers, and/or students

  • Must align with the scientific objectives outlined in the proposal

Regional Eligibility Rules

IBRO applies specific regional collaboration guidelines:

  • Africa, Asia-Pacific, Pan-Europe: Project leaders must apply with an inter-regional collaborator (outside their IBRO region).

  • Latin America: Eligible for both intra-regional and inter-regional collaborations.

  • US/Canada: Eligible for intra-regional (US–Canada) or inter-regional collaborations. Inter-regional partnerships are preferred.

Special consideration is given to proposals that strengthen emerging groups, smaller institutions, and underrepresented research environments.

Why Apply for IBRO Collaborative Research Grants?

  • Expand your international research network

  • Strengthen capacity in emerging neuroscience labs

  • Gain global research visibility

  • Support mobility of early-career researchers

  • Foster long-term scientific partnerships

For neuroscientists looking to build sustainable international collaborations, this grant provides structured support and recognition from a globally respected scientific organization.

How to Apply

  1. Create an applicant account on the IBRO Grants Portal

  2. Review the funding terms and FAQ

  3. Submit your application during the official application window

Applicants are encouraged to review all eligibility criteria carefully before submission.

For more information, visit the official IBRO website: Click Here

Check for more grant opportunities here

The post IBRO Collaborative Research Grants 2026: Up to $6,000 to Fund International Neuroscience Collaborations appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Digital Gender Inclusion and Innovation Bootcamp 2026: Free UNHCR Programme for Women-Led and Refugee-Led Organisations
item.dc:creator: Anna Gurung
item.description:
Women and girls who are forcibly displaced or stateless often face significant barriers to accessing technology, digital tools, and online opportunities. To address this urgent global challenge, UNHCR, the UN Refugee Agency, in partnership with Here I Am Studio, has launched the Digital Gender Inclusion and Innovation Bootcamp 2026. This fully funded online bootcamp is […]

The post Digital Gender Inclusion and Innovation Bootcamp 2026: Free UNHCR Programme for Women-Led and Refugee-Led Organisations appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Women and girls who are forcibly displaced or stateless often face significant barriers to accessing technology, digital tools, and online opportunities. To address this urgent global challenge, UNHCR, the UN Refugee Agency, in partnership with Here I Am Studio, has launched the Digital Gender Inclusion and Innovation Bootcamp 2026.

This fully funded online bootcamp is designed specifically for women-led and refugee-led organisations working with displaced and stateless women and girls. The programme aims to strengthen digital gender equality, advance inclusive innovation, and support safe, meaningful access to technology.

Application Deadline: 16 March 2026
Bootcamp Dates: 08 April 2026 – 22 April 2026

About the Digital Gender Inclusion and Innovation Bootcamp 2026

The Digital Gender Inclusion and Innovation Bootcamp 2026 is part of UNHCR’s broader commitment to:

  • Strengthening the leadership and agency of displaced and stateless women

  • Building inclusive and community-driven innovation ecosystems

  • Advancing digital gender equality

  • Supporting safe and meaningful digital access

Through this programme, selected organisations will gain practical tools, strategic insights, and applied skills to close the digital gender gap in their communities.

What the Bootcamp Offers

This free online programme provides a blended learning experience combining live sessions and self-paced modules.

Participants Will Receive:

  • 5 live online learning sessions

  • 8 self-paced online learning modules

  • Peer learning, exchange, and networking opportunities

  • A Digital Gender Inclusion and Innovation Bootcamp Certificate upon successful completion

Participants will develop knowledge and practical skills in:

  • Online safety and digital protection for women and girls

  • Using digital tools for protection and accountability

  • Developing digital livelihoods for economic empowerment

  • Human-centred design

  • Project management

  • Partnership-building and collaboration

The programme provides a holistic approach to delivering impactful digital gender inclusion initiatives in displacement contexts.

Who Can Apply?

Applications are open to organisations working with forcibly displaced and stateless women and girls, including:

  • Women-led organisations (WLOs), especially refugee women-led organisations (RWLOs)

  • Refugee-led organisations (RLOs)

  • Community-based organisations (CBOs)

Organisational Requirements

Eligible organisations must:

  • Have experience supporting displaced or stateless women and girls and/or demonstrate a strong commitment to advancing their rights and opportunities

  • Have experience in digital inclusion and innovation work or show a clear interest in building knowledge in this area

  • Nominate up to two women participants from their organisation

Participant Requirements

Nominated participants must:

  • Commit 10–12 total hours during the programme

    • 5–6 hours for live online sessions

    • 5–6 hours for self-paced modules

  • Speak English, French, Arabic, or Spanish

  • Have access to Zoom (via computer or mobile phone)

  • Have a stable internet connection

  • Be comfortable using basic online learning platforms

Live sessions are exclusively for women participants to ensure safe and meaningful engagement.

Accessibility and Inclusion

The organisers will make every effort to accommodate selected organisations, including participants with visual or hearing impairments. A detailed agenda tailored to applicant priorities will be shared with selected participants.

How to Apply

Interested organisations must complete the official application form by:

Deadline: 16 March 2026

The bootcamp will run from 08 April 2026 to 22 April 2026.

For questions, applicants may contact: unhcr2026bootcamp@hereiamstudio.com

This is a unique opportunity to help ensure that the digital age leaves no displaced or stateless woman behind. Organisations working at the forefront of community-based impact are strongly encouraged to apply.

For more information CLICK HERE

For more bootcamp opportunity check HERE

The post Digital Gender Inclusion and Innovation Bootcamp 2026: Free UNHCR Programme for Women-Led and Refugee-Led Organisations appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Up to $160,000 EdTech Research Grants for Foundational Learning in Kenya, Malawi, and Senegal (Apply by 2 March 2026)
item.dc:creator: Anna Gurung
item.description:
Education research organisations in Kenya, Malawi, and Senegal now have an opportunity to secure up to $160,000 in funding to strengthen evidence-informed EdTech policy and improve foundational learning outcomes. The call is issued under the African EdTech Initiative (AEI), a three-year research programme funded by the International Development Research Centre (IDRC) and implemented by Education […]

The post Up to $160,000 EdTech Research Grants for Foundational Learning in Kenya, Malawi, and Senegal (Apply by 2 March 2026) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Education research organisations in Kenya, Malawi, and Senegal now have an opportunity to secure up to $160,000 in funding to strengthen evidence-informed EdTech policy and improve foundational learning outcomes.

The call is issued under the African EdTech Initiative (AEI), a three-year research programme funded by the International Development Research Centre (IDRC) and implemented by Education Sub Saharan Africa (ESSA), EdTech Hub, and Association for the Development of Education in Africa (ADEA).

This competitive funding aims to empower local research organisations to collaborate directly with Ministries of Education (MoEs) and shape national EdTech strategies that improve foundational learning for all learners.

Application Deadline

Deadline: Monday, 2 March 2026, 9:00 AM GMT

Late submissions will not be considered.

About the African EdTech Initiative (AEI)

The African EdTech Initiative seeks to answer a critical policy question:

How can governments in sub-Saharan Africa coordinate EdTech research to enable strategic, effective, and cost-efficient improvements in foundational learning?

The programme works closely with Ministries of Education and local research organisations to generate context-specific, policy-relevant evidence that strengthens national EdTech systems.

Through this grant, selected organisations will co-lead country-level action research in partnership with their national Ministry of Education.

Grant Overview

  • Grant Amount: Up to $160,000

  • Duration: Approximately 18 months

  • Eligible Countries: Kenya, Malawi, Senegal

  • Number of Grants: One per country

Each grant will fund:

  • Research design and implementation

  • Coordination with the Ministry of Education

  • Policy engagement activities

  • Dissemination of findings

The goal is not only to produce research but to ensure findings are integrated into national policy processes and EdTech implementation plans focused on foundational learning.

What This Funding Supports

Selected research organisations will:

  • Identify priority EdTech research questions with Ministries

  • Co-design and implement country-level EdTech studies

  • Strengthen government capacity to coordinate research agendas

  • Support evidence-informed policymaking

  • Promote sustainable improvements in foundational learning

This initiative prioritises local ownership, context-driven solutions, and strong government partnerships.

Eligibility Criteria

To qualify, applicants must:

  • Be a legally registered entity (university, NGO, research institute, think tank, or private research firm)

  • Be operational in Kenya, Malawi, or Senegal

  • Demonstrate experience in EdTech research with a focus on foundational learning

  • Show capacity for robust, ethical, and policy-relevant research

  • Demonstrate experience working with Ministries of Education or relevant public sector stakeholders

  • Show commitment to gender inclusivity and diversity

Applicants must also demonstrate understanding of national education systems, curricula, and policymaking processes related to EdTech and foundational learning.

Why This Grant Matters

  • Strengthens local research leadership

  • Builds Ministry capacity to coordinate EdTech research

  • Influences national education policy

  • Promotes evidence-based innovation in foundational learning

  • Contributes to long-term improvements in education systems

For research organisations committed to improving learning outcomes through technology, this is a high-impact funding opportunity.

How to Apply

Interested organisations should:

  • Review the Call for Proposals

  • Read the Frequently Asked Questions

  • Complete the official Application Form

Applications must be submitted before 2 March 2026 at 9:00 AM GMT.

For full details and application access Click Here

For more grant opportunities check Here

The post Up to $160,000 EdTech Research Grants for Foundational Learning in Kenya, Malawi, and Senegal (Apply by 2 March 2026) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: UNIDIR Graduate Professional Programme 2026: Paid Peace and Security Roles in Geneva and Remote (Apply by 2 March 2026)
item.dc:creator: Anna Gurung
item.description:
The United Nations Institute for Disarmament Research (UNIDIR) is inviting applications for two Graduate Professional (Conventional Arms and Ammunition) positions for 2026. This opportunity is ideal for graduate students and recent graduates passionate about international peace, security, and disarmament. Based within the United Nations system and headquartered at the Palais des Nations in Geneva, UNIDIR […]

The post UNIDIR Graduate Professional Programme 2026: Paid Peace and Security Roles in Geneva and Remote (Apply by 2 March 2026) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The United Nations Institute for Disarmament Research (UNIDIR) is inviting applications for two Graduate Professional (Conventional Arms and Ammunition) positions for 2026. This opportunity is ideal for graduate students and recent graduates passionate about international peace, security, and disarmament.

Based within the United Nations system and headquartered at the Palais des Nations in Geneva, UNIDIR conducts independent research on disarmament and global security challenges. These six-month positions offer hands-on experience supporting high-level research and policy work related to conventional arms control.

Application Deadline

The deadline to apply is midnight Geneva time on 2 March 2026.

Position Overview

Position Title: Graduate Professional (Conventional Arms and Ammunition) – 2 Positions
Start Date: 20 April 2026 (Negotiable)
Duration: 6 months
Occupational Rate: 100% (Negotiable)
Location:

  • 1 Remote position (Research Pillar 2: Preventing Armed Conflict and Armed Violence)

  • 1 Geneva-based position (Exploratory Research Cell)

Applicants must clearly indicate their preferred position in the subject line of their application email.

Stipend and Financial Information

Graduate Professionals will receive:

  • USD 1,331 per month (in-person, Geneva-based)

  • USD 665.50 per month (remote)

The stipend is intended as a contribution toward living expenses. Travel, visas, health insurance, accommodation, and related costs are the responsibility of the Graduate Professional or their sponsoring institution.

About the Conventional Arms and Ammunition Programme

UNIDIR’s programme works to prevent and mitigate the uncontrolled proliferation and misuse of conventional arms and ammunition. It contributes to sustainable peace and security through:

  • Research on weapons and ammunition management

  • Prevention of armed conflict and armed violence

  • Multi-stakeholder dialogues

  • Advisory support and capacity-building

The Graduate Professionals will support either:

  • Research Pillar 2: Preventing Armed Conflict and Armed Violence (Remote)

  • Exploratory Research Cell (Geneva-based)

Key Responsibilities

Selected candidates will:

  • Conduct background research and analysis

  • Support planning and implementation of meetings, workshops, and conferences

  • Assist in administrative coordination and monitoring

  • Draft concise research briefs and documents

This role provides exposure to international policy research, UN processes, and global security governance.

Eligibility Criteria

Education

Applicants must:

  • Have graduated within the past year with a university degree (Bachelor’s or Master’s equivalent), OR

  • Be currently enrolled in a graduate programme

The role is intended to be full-time.

Preferred academic fields include:

  • Politics

  • International Affairs

  • Peace and Conflict Studies

  • Security Studies

  • International Law

Experience

  • Professional work experience is not required

  • Interest or experience in conventional arms control is an asset

  • Research, analytical, and writing skills are essential

Language Requirements

  • Fluency in English (oral and written) is required

  • Knowledge of French or Spanish is desirable

How to Apply

Applicants must submit the following as one single PDF document titled:

SURNAME_Name_PHP-CL

Include:

  • Cover letter

  • Inspira Personal History Form (PHP)

  • Responses (maximum 30 words each) to screening questions

Send applications to:
unidir.recruitment@un.org

Subject line must state either:

“UNIDIR Conventional Arms and Ammunition GPP – Remote”
OR
“UNIDIR Conventional Arms and Ammunition GPP – Geneva-based”

Incomplete, late, or AI-generated applications will not be processed.

Why Apply?

  • Gain direct experience within the United Nations system

  • Contribute to global disarmament and peacebuilding research

  • Work alongside international experts in Geneva or remotely

  • Strengthen your profile for careers in international security

For aspiring professionals in peace and security, this is a highly valuable early-career opportunity within a respected UN research institution.

For more information, visit the official UNIDIR website: Click Here

Check for more job opportunities here

The post UNIDIR Graduate Professional Programme 2026: Paid Peace and Security Roles in Geneva and Remote (Apply by 2 March 2026) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Apply for the UNU-MERIT Knowledge Management for Young Professionals Online Course
item.dc:creator: Susan Kamene
item.description:
Are you a Bachelor’s or Master’s student looking to supercharge your career skills in knowledge management? The United Nations University–MERIT (UNU-MERIT) is offering an exclusive, tuition-free 2-week online course designed to help you master how to organise, manage, and apply knowledge effectively — a skill highly sought in research, policy, consultancy, and international development careers. […]

The post Apply for the UNU-MERIT Knowledge Management for Young Professionals Online Course appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Are you a Bachelor’s or Master’s student looking to supercharge your career skills in knowledge management? The United Nations University–MERIT (UNU-MERIT) is offering an exclusive, tuition-free 2-week online course designed to help you master how to organise, manage, and apply knowledge effectively — a skill highly sought in research, policy, consultancy, and international development careers.

What Is the UNU-MERIT Knowledge Management Course?

This dynamic online course offers practical tools and insights into knowledge management for professionals in training — helping you:

  • Understand the importance of knowledge in professional and academic contexts.

  • Learn techniques to capture, structure, and communicate knowledge.

  • Gain skills applicable in research, development projects, and workplace environments.

Course Organiser: United Nations University–Maastricht Economic and Social Research Institute on Innovation and Technology (UNU-MERIT) — a UN research and capacity development institute focused on innovation and sustainable development.

Benefits of the Course

 Professional Skills Boost

This UNU-MERIT course focuses on building practical, career-relevant knowledge management skills, which are invaluable in:

  • Research environments

  • Public policy and governance roles

  • Consultancy and development agencies

  • Academic and professional writing

Global Certificate & Exposure

Upon completion, you will have:

• Enhanced understanding of how to organise and apply knowledge
• A UNU-certified online course on your academic/professional profile
• Exposure to international learning environments and best practices

Strategic Career Advantages

This course adds marketable expertise to your skill set that employers in development, research, and policy fields actively look for. With the world becoming more data-driven, knowing how to manage and leverage knowledge effectively sets you apart from peers.

Eligibility & Who Should Apply

Who can apply:

  • Current Bachelor’s students

  • Current Master’s students

  • Young professionals interested in knowledge management

  • Individuals seeking career development skills

There is no tuition fee, making this a cost-free professional development opportunity.

How to Apply

Here’s what you need to know:

Apply Online:
Direct Application Link click here

Note: Make sure your application is complete before the deadline — include all required information requested on the application form.

Key Dates & Deadlines

Milestone Date / Time (CET)
Application Deadline 11 March 2026 at 12 PM CET
Course Dates 16 – 26 March 2026

Important: Late submissions will not be accepted — apply before the deadline to be considered.

UNU-MERIT is a Maastricht-based institute of the United Nations University dedicated to innovation, public policy research, and capacity development. It runs postgraduate programmes, research projects, and professional development courses worldwide.

For more information visit here

For more global opportunities click here

The post Apply for the UNU-MERIT Knowledge Management for Young Professionals Online Course appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: International Excellence Scholarships at University of Greater Manchester
item.dc:creator: Susan Kamene
item.description:
If you’re an ambitious international student dreaming of studying abroad with financial support, this is your ultimate guide to the 2026/27 International Excellence Scholarships offered by the University of Greater Manchester International Scholarships. Why This Scholarship Could Be a Game-Changer The International Excellence Scholarships at the University of Greater Manchester are designed to recognize top […]

The post International Excellence Scholarships at University of Greater Manchester appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
If you’re an ambitious international student dreaming of studying abroad with financial support, this is your ultimate guide to the 2026/27 International Excellence Scholarships offered by the University of Greater Manchester International Scholarships.

Why This Scholarship Could Be a Game-Changer

The International Excellence Scholarships at the University of Greater Manchester are designed to recognize top academic merit and reward talented global applicants — helping reduce tuition costs and support your degree pursuit in the UK.

Whether you’re planning to pursue an Undergraduate, Postgraduate Taught, or PhD programme, there’s a range of scholarships tailored to various levels and abilities.

Scholarship Types & Benefits

 International Excellence Scholarships

This core scheme offers tuition fee discounts for Year 1 only, based on academic merit.

 Award Levels

1. Global Excellence Package
Available to self-funded applicants from selected countries (including Ghana, Kenya, South Africa, UAE, China, USA, Canada, Malaysia, etc.):

  • Gold Award – £4,000 tuition discount
    (First class or 70% GPA or equivalent)

  • Silver Award – £3,000 tuition discount
    (Upper second or 60% GPA)

  • Bronze Award – £2,000 tuition discount

 Eligible for Undergraduate, Postgraduate Taught & PhD programmes.

2. International Merit Package
For students from all other countries or via an agent:

  • Gold – £3,000

  • Silver – Up to £2,000

  • Bronze – Up to £1,000

 Applies to Undergraduate, Postgraduate and PhD study.

Global Scholars Awards

These are prestigious and highly competitive scholarships:

  • 10 awards of £5,000 tuition discount (Year 1)

  •  One outstanding recipient becomes The Chancellor’s Excellence Scholar with a 100% tuition fee waiver for Year 1

  • Separate application required with interview step

  • Must be eligible for the Gold International Excellence Award and apply for September 2026 programmes

Who Is Eligible?

To qualify for these scholarships:

• International fee-paying students
• Hold an offer for an eligible programme (UG, PG, PhD)
• Be self-funded (government-sponsored students not eligible)
• Meet academic excellence criteria (GPA thresholds vary)

Additional conditions include:
– One’s nationality and application route (direct vs agent) can affect eligibility.

Application Process & Submission Tips

  1. Apply for Your Degree Programme at the University of Greater Manchester first.

    • You MUST have an offer before scholarship consideration.

  2. Check Scholarship Eligibility based on your nationality and academic credentials.

  3. Submit Additional Documents (if required for Global Scholars).

    • Personal statement

    • Evidence of academic potential

    • Leadership / impact statements

  4. Attend Interview (for Global Scholars applicants).

  5. Follow Up via University communications to confirm decisions.

Note: The scholarship is applied at enrolment and cannot be deferred or exchanged for cash.

Apply here

Why Apply? Benefits Explained

• Reduce Tuition Cost – Significant fee discounts in Year 1
• Reward for Excellence – Recognizes your academic achievements
• Enhances Your CV – Prestigious scholarship recognition
• Support for Global Students – Encourages leadership and academic ambition

Key Deadlines (2026/27 Entry)

Important Dates to Remember

Event Deadline
Scholarship Application Opens Already open for 2026/27 admissions
Deadline for Global Scholars Awards Before main admissions deadline (check with Admissions)
Programme Start Date September 2026

Note: Specific deadlines aren’t always explicitly listed — applicants are encouraged to check scholarship decisions timelines with admissions or international office after receiving an offer.

Additional Awards You Should Know

These can be stacked with excellence awards to further reduce tuition costs:

  • Women in STEM Award – £500

  • Family Discount – £500

  • Transnational Education (TNE) Progression Award – £500

 All additional awards are applied at the point of CAS issuance and subject to terms.

For more information visit here

For more global opportunities click here

The post International Excellence Scholarships at University of Greater Manchester appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Oxford Blavatnik School of Government Pre-Doctoral Research Assistant 2026
item.dc:creator: Susan Kamene
item.description:
If you’re a high-achieving graduate or early-career researcher with a passion for economics and public policy, the Pre-Doctoral Research Assistant (PDRA) position at the University of Oxford’s Blavatnik School of Government (BSG) is one of the most prestigious stepping stones to a research, policy, or academic career. Role Overview: What You’ll Do As a Pre-Doctoral […]

The post Oxford Blavatnik School of Government Pre-Doctoral Research Assistant 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
If you’re a high-achieving graduate or early-career researcher with a passion for economics and public policy, the Pre-Doctoral Research Assistant (PDRA) position at the University of Oxford’s Blavatnik School of Government (BSG) is one of the most prestigious stepping stones to a research, policy, or academic career.

Role Overview: What You’ll Do

As a Pre-Doctoral Research Assistant in Economics and Public Policy, you will:

  • Support high-impact research projects in public policy and economics.

  • Work with large administrative datasets (e.g., tax, innovation, patents).

  • Help design research projects, analyze data, and contribute to academic outputs.

  • Gain unique exposure to cutting-edge social science and policy research methods.

This position provides a rare opportunity to build strong research capabilities ahead of doctoral studies or professional research roles in policy institutions.

Eligibility & Qualifications

To qualify for this competitive role, candidates should meet the following core requirements:

1. Academic Background

  • Bachelor’s degree (or equivalent) in Economics, Public Policy, Finance, or a related quantitative discipline.

2. Skill Requirements

  • Strong quantitative and analytical skills.

  • Evidence of working with data or research tools (e.g., statistical software).

  • Demonstrated interest in public policy research or economic analysis.

3. Professional Attributes

  • Excellent communication skills.

  • Ability to work independently and collaboratively.

  • Eagerness to contribute to high-quality international research.

Benefits of the PDRA Role

This role is more than just a job — it’s a career accelerator:

1. Professional & Academic Growth

  • Hands-on experience with policy-relevant economic research.

  • Exposure to Oxford’s leading faculty and research culture.

  • Significant advantage for pursuing a DPhil/PhD or policy leadership roles.

2. Work Benefits

According to the official job posting:

  • Excellent contributory pension scheme.

  • 38 days’ annual leave per year.

  • Family-friendly leave and childcare services.

  • Cycle loan scheme and discounted travel options.

  • Opportunities for hybrid working and access to university resources.

These benefits make this role particularly attractive for early-career researchers seeking work-life balance alongside professional development.

Where You’ll Be Based

Blavatnik School of Government — part of the University of Oxford

  • Located in Oxford, UK — a globally renowned center of research excellence.

  • Outstanding facilities, academic network, and vibrant intellectual environment.

Students and staff enjoy access to libraries, lecture series, and international policy networks.

How to Apply — Step-by-Step

  1. Prepare Your CV & Supporting Statement
    Explain your academic excellence, research experience, and how you meet the job criteria.

  2. Gather References
    Two academic or professional referees should be ready to support your application.

  3. Submit Online Before Deadline
    Use the official Oxford jobs portal here

  4. Complete Interview Stage
    Shortlisted candidates will be interviewed in late March 2026.

Insider Tips for a Competitive Application

• Highlight Quantitative Skills – Emphasize any relevant experience with statistical tools or research methods.

• Public Policy Insight – Showcase meaningful engagement with public policy issues or research projects.

• Leadership & Initiative – Demonstrate analytical leadership and how you can contribute to high-quality research.

• Network with Faculty – If possible, engage with Oxford researchers — referrals can strengthen your profile.

Key Application Deadlines & Timeline (2026)

Milestone Deadline (UK Time)
Application Submission – PDRA 12:00 noon, 17 March 2026
Interview Round Week commencing 30 March 2026
Position Start Post-selection as advised (typical summer/early autumn)

Make sure to apply before noon (UK time) on the closing date — late submissions will not be accepted.

The Pre-Doctoral Research Assistant role at Oxford’s Blavatnik School of Government is more than just employment — it’s a gateway into elite global research networks, influential policy circles, and future doctoral study. With the 17 March 2026 deadline approaching, act fast, prepare your application meticulously, and leverage this opportunity to launch a high-impact career.

For more information visit here

For more global opportunities click here

The post Oxford Blavatnik School of Government Pre-Doctoral Research Assistant 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Apply Now: Erasmus Mundus Visiting Scholarships for Academics and Policy Professionals 2026-2027 — Fully Funded, Prestigious European Opportunity with Competitive Stipends
item.dc:creator: Susan Kamene
item.description:
Are you an accomplished academic, policy researcher, or experienced practitioner with a PhD? Don’t miss your chance to join the highly respected Erasmus Mundus Visiting Scholarships for Academics & Policy Professionals hosted by the internationally acclaimed Mundus MAPP Consortium. This prestigious European scholarship program offers short-term (2 – 4 week) funded placements at top institutions […]

The post Apply Now: Erasmus Mundus Visiting Scholarships for Academics and Policy Professionals 2026-2027 — Fully Funded, Prestigious European Opportunity with Competitive Stipends appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Are you an accomplished academic, policy researcher, or experienced practitioner with a PhD? Don’t miss your chance to join the highly respected Erasmus Mundus Visiting Scholarships for Academics & Policy Professionals hosted by the internationally acclaimed Mundus MAPP Consortium.

This prestigious European scholarship program offers short-term (2 – 4 week) funded placements at top institutions across Austria, the Netherlands, Spain, and the United Kingdom — with real impact on teaching, research, and policy engagement.

Why This Scholarship Matters 

Erasmus Mundus Visiting Scholarships provide a unique professional development platform for scholars and policy practitioners to:

  • Deliver courses, seminars, public lectures, and research workshops;

  • Participate in core academic and thesis supervision activities;

  • Engage with international networks of students and faculty;

  • Gain experience across leading European universities;

  • Expand research collaborations and publication opportunities.

Scholarship Benefits & Financial Support

The European Commission funds the Visiting Scholarships, and scholars receive:

Financial Package

  • €1,500 gross per week stipend

  • Up to €6,000 maximum total support (for the entire visit)

Please note:Apply Now: Erasmus Mundus Visiting Scholarships for Academics and Policy Professionals 2026-2027 — Fully Funded, Prestigious European Opportunity with Competitive Stipends

  • Scholars are responsible for understanding and complying with tax obligations.

  • Additional benefits and reimbursements may vary by host institution (e.g., workshop honoraria at CEU).

Who Can Apply? Eligibility & Qualifications

To qualify for the Erasmus Mundus Visiting Scholarships:

Essential Criteria

  • PhD degree completed (in social sciences, public policy, governance, political economy, or related fields).

  • Proven academic, research, or policy practice experience relevant to public policy and governance.

  • Open to academics and policy professionals of ALL NATIONALITIES.

Preferred Contributions

Your application should include a detailed plan of how you intend to contribute to Mundus MAPP, whether through:

  • Teaching seminars or lectures

  • Leading research discussions

  • Supporting thesis supervision

  • Providing training in policy analysis tools

  • Hosting short workshops or online sessions

Mundus MAPP alumni are strongly encouraged to apply.

How to Apply — Step-by-Step

  1. Download the Application Form
    Use the official form linked on the Mundus MAPP website.

  2. Prepare Supporting Documents
    Include a CV, list of publications, proposed contribution outline, and supporting letters if available.

  3. Submit by Email
    Send your complete application to: mundusmapp@ceu.edu

  4. Follow Up
    Direct queries to mundusmapp@ceu.edu — allow time for responses, as queries are high during peak seasons.

No online portal submission — this call requires email application only.

Apply here

Tips for a Winning Application (SEO-Friendly Advice)

• Highlight your academic or policy impact — concrete examples strengthen your profile.
• Tailor your contribution plan to the host institution’s strengths.
• Include clear outcomes of how your visit will benefit students and faculty.
• Early submission boosts review time before the deadline.

Important Deadlines & Visiting Periods

Application Deadline:
15th May 2026 — All applications must be submitted by this date via email.

Visiting Period Windows:
Scholars can spend 2–4 weeks at one of the Mundus MAPP partner institutions within the following windows:

Central European University PU (Austria)

  • January 4 – June 11, 2027

 International Institute of Social Studies (Erasmus University Rotterdam, Netherlands)

  • May 1 – May 31, 2027

 Institut Barcelona d’Estudis Internacionals (Spain)

  • October 15 – December 15, 2026

 University of York (UK)

  • September 21, 2026 – June 11, 2027

The Erasmus Mundus Visiting Scholarship is a rare, fully funded opportunity for international academics and policy professionals. Position yourself at the forefront of European public policy education and global research collaboration.

For more information visit here

For more global opportunities click here

The post Apply Now: Erasmus Mundus Visiting Scholarships for Academics and Policy Professionals 2026-2027 — Fully Funded, Prestigious European Opportunity with Competitive Stipends appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
